title: Stop-and-Wait Protocol
subtitle: Sliding Window Protocol with Window Size 1
description: Implementation of the Stop-and-Wait ARQ protocol

theory.overview: Stop-and-Wait is the simplest flow control protocol. Imagine throwing a ball to a friend. You wait for them to catch it and say 'Got it!' before you throw the next one. If they don't say anything after a few seconds, you assume they dropped it and throw another one.

key_concepts.definition: A small message sent by the receiver to confirm a packet was successfully received.
A timer started by the sender. If an ACK isn't received before the timer expires, the packet is retransmitted.
A number attached to each packet (usually 0 or 1 in Stop-and-Wait) to distinguish new packets from retransmissions.

protocol_flow: Step 1: Sender transmits Packet 0 and starts a timer.
Step 2: Receiver gets Packet 0 and sends ACK 0.
Step 3: Sender receives ACK 0, stops timer, and transmits Packet 1.
Step 4: If timer expires before ACK arrives, sender retransmits the packet.

steps.title: Initialize Frames
steps.explanation: We define an array of frames to send. `n` is the total number of frames, and `i` keeps track of the current frame being sent. `ack` will store the simulated acknowledgement status.

steps.title: Send Loop
steps.explanation: The `while` loop continues until all `n` frames are successfully sent and acknowledged. Inside, we print that we are sending the current frame and waiting for its ACK.

steps.title: Simulate Network
steps.explanation: We use `sleep(1)` to simulate the time it takes for a packet to travel across the network. Then, we use `rand() % 2` to randomly simulate whether the ACK was successfully received (1) or lost/delayed (0).

steps.title: Handle ACK or Timeout
steps.explanation: If `ack == 1`, the frame was successfully received, so we increment `i` to move to the next frame. If `ack == 0`, we simulate a timeout. We don't increment `i`, so the loop will retransmit the same frame in the next iteration.

exercises.description: What should happen when an ACK is received?
exercises.hint: You need to move to the next frame.
